title: “We Have Antigravity UFOs!” (ft. Nick Cook)
summary: Nick Cook, former aviation editor for Jane's Defence Weekly, discusses his investigation into aerospace industry's secretive anti-gravity research, documented in his book 'The Hunt for Zero Point.' The interview covers Nazi Germany's rumored advanced propulsion programs under SS officer Hans Kammler, the work of inventors like Thomas Townsend Brown and Ning Li, and the possibility that some UFOs may be man-made craft utilizing breakthrough physics.

acronyms.definition: Martin Corporation's research division that studied anti-gravity under George Trimble
Australia's intelligence organization, essentially their CIA equivalent
Air Force investigative unit that produced declassified UFO reports

organizations_mentioned: University of North Carolina Chapel Hill

events.description: Philadelphia Experiment allegedly conducted involving USS Eldridge
Rudolph Schriever claims disc-shaped craft made first test flight in Prague, climbing to 40,000 feet in 3 minutes
General Nathan Twining writes the Twining Memo stating UFOs are real and not visionary or fictitious
Stanley Deser and Richard Arnowitt win Gravity Research Foundation essay on gravitons
Article 'The G Engines Are Coming' published describing aerospace industry's anti-gravity research
Chapel Hill conference on gravity held, sponsored by Wright Airfield, kicking off Golden Age of relativity
Thomas Townsend Brown demonstrates fan precipitator to Edward Teller at Teller's Berkeley home
Brown shuts down Guidance Technologies company claiming no results
Northrop publishes paper on electroaerodynamics and electrogravitics three months after Brown closes Guidance Technologies
Australian JIO intelligence memo written by Harry Turner listing aerospace companies and physicists involved in gravity research
Ben Rich takes over Skunk Works from Kelly Johnson
Nick Cook receives mysterious 1956 anti-gravity article on his desk at Jane's Defence Weekly
Nick Cook interviews Ning Li at MIT Corporation conference near Washington DC
NASA patents filed for asymmetric capacitor with Larry Smalley listed
CCP approaches Ning Li to continue her work in China; she refuses and is banned from the country
Ning Li hit by car causing brain impairment; her husband dies of heart attack from shock

key_claims.claim: George Trimble stated human control of gravity could be achieved in about the time it took to build the first atom bomb
George Trimble refused to speak to Nick Cook and seemed scared when contacted about anti-gravity research
General Twining's 1947 memo stated it is within present knowledge to build disc-shaped craft with extreme performance characteristics
Ben Rich ended a talk with a slide of a black disc and said 'We now have the technology to take ET home'
Ben Rich stated on his deathbed that we already have the means to travel among the stars but these technologies are locked up in black projects
Paul Mellon told his grandson John Warner IV that he and Allen Dulles saw a German flying disc in a facility in Czechoslovakia at the end of WWII
Ning Li's experiments showed test masses lost between 0.5% and 2.1% of their weight when suspended above spinning superconductors
The B2 stealth bomber's performance data does not add up given the power of its engines according to Bill Gunston
Aviation Weekly reported the B2 uses an electrokinetic effect producing a Biefeld-Brown effect
Edward Teller said 'I don't know how this works' when shown Townsend Brown's fan precipitator demonstration
Secrets can keep for 35 to 40 years when special measures like disinformation are employed
Aerospace and defense industry is prepared to go further in looking at heretical science than mainstream science itself
Ben Rich referred to UFOs as 'unfunded opportunities'
The 1971 Australian intelligence memo lists Robert Oppenheimer as being actively involved in anti-gravity research
